response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Honestly, talking about COVID vaccines and healthcare stuff makes me really anxious. I spend a lot of time on social media and you see so many different opinions flying around that it becomes overwhelming. Everyone seems so certain about their position, and when discussions start, they can get heated really fast. As someone who works with my hands building things, I prefer situations where there are clear answers and proven methods, not all this conflicting information that changes every few months. When these conversations come up at work sites or even with friends, I try to keep things simple and avoid getting too deep into debates. I usually just say that I trust what the doctors in Bologna recommended and leave it at that. The problem is that people online especially seem to want you to pick a side immediately and defend it passionately, which just stresses me out. I already deal with enough worry in my daily life without adding more fuel to that fire. What helps me is setting boundaries in these discussions. If someone starts getting aggressive or pushy about their views on vaccines, I just politely say I'm not comfortable going into all the details and try to change the subject. Sometimes I'll mention that healthcare is complicated and I'm not an expert, so I prefer to follow what health professionals suggest rather than debate it endlessly. My approach might seem like avoiding the issue, but for my mental health, it's necessary. I've noticed that when I engage too much in these arguments, especially online, I end up feeling drained and worried for hours afterward. Instead, I focus my energy on things I can control, like my carpentry work or tending to my garden. Those activities calm me down and give me a sense of accomplishment that endless healthcare debates never do. I think it's okay to admit when a topic is beyond your expertise and causes you stress, and to simply step back from those conversations when they're not productive or necessary for your immediate life.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona has high neuroticism (79) which means they experience anxiety and stress easily, especially in confrontational situations. Their low openness (21) suggests they prefer familiar approaches and clear-cut solutions rather than engaging with novel or complex debates. Spending several hours daily on social media exposes them to many conflicting opinions about COVID vaccines. Their moderate conscientiousness and agreeableness mean they try to be reasonable but aren't overly accommodating. As a self-employed carpenter with lower secondary education, they likely prefer practical, hands-on solutions over abstract debates. Their Hindu beliefs might inform some health perspectives but aren't necessarily central to vaccine discussions. Being widowed at 23 adds emotional burden, contributing to stress avoidance. The tone should be honest, somewhat anxious, practical, and focused on self-preservation rather than engaging in debates.
response_parsed.tone: anxious, practical, honest, stress-avoidant, moderately informal
